process_step.details: NOTE: Only process documents with route_to_step=5 (document_category="Site Plan") — the same document set as step 5. For each Site Plan document processed in step 5, extract the complete drawing legend (key) from every page using the vision extraction pipeline defined in proc-extract-site-plan-info step 4. Scan every image chunk of each drawing for a LEGEND, KEY, NOTATION, or SYMBOL TABLE panel. For each visual element in the legend extract: a plain-English description of the symbol or line style, the exact text label as written on the drawing, a category (cable/equipment/substation/boundary/earthing/annotation/other), and the page number it was found on. Assemble all entries across all chunks and all pages into a single structured list per document.

expected_output.description: Step 4: Extract Drawing Legend — A structured legend extraction per document: a JSON array of legend entries, each with symbol description, label, category, and page number.

fields.sub-step-extract-legend: JSON array of legend entry objects — each with 'page' (integer), 'symbol_description' (string — visual geometry description of the drawn symbol, e.g. 'Small open circle with lines radiating outward' NOT a label paraphrase like 'New lantern'), 'label' (string — verbatim label text from the drawing), 'category' (string)

sub_steps.sub_step_name: Extract Legend Entries from All Pages
sub_steps.details: Scan every image chunk of the drawing for a legend, key, notation, or symbol table. For each row in the legend, extract: (1) the VISUAL GEOMETRY of the drawn symbol — describe its exact shape, fill, line weight, line pattern, and any interior marks as you see them drawn; (2) the exact text label; (3) the page number. CRITICAL RULE: symbol_description must describe what the symbol LOOKS LIKE as a drawn graphic, NOT a paraphrase or echo of the label text. Each legend row has its own unique symbol — do not assign the same visual description to multiple rows. If you see the same description repeating for consecutive rows, you have misread the legend — look again at each row individually. WRONG examples (label paraphrases — never produce these): 'New lantern', 'Existing pole', 'New pillar', 'Remove conductor', 'Underground cable'. CORRECT examples (visual geometry): 'Small hollow circle with short lines radiating outward from the edge', 'Small solid black filled circle', 'Hollow rectangle outline with no fill', 'Solid thin line with evenly spaced short perpendicular tick marks'. REFERENCE TABLE — use these known symbol descriptions when you recognise the visual pattern next to the label: NEW LV TRENCH → Long dashed line; STRING NEW OH CABLE → Short dashed line; EXISTING UNDERGROUND MAINS → Alternating long-short dashed line; EXISTING OH CABLE → Solid thin line; REMOVE CONDUCTOR → Dotted line; EXISTING DUCTS → Thick heavy solid black line; NEW HV TRENCH → Solid line with two diagonal slash marks; EXISTING LANTERN → Small circle with internal cross (four-quadrant cross inside circle); REMOVE LANTERN → Circle with internal cross and starburst outer spikes radiating from edge; NEW LANTERN → Circle with starburst outer spikes and hollow centre; EXISTING POLE → Small solid black filled circle; REMOVE POLE → Small solid black filled circle with large X through it; REPLACE POLE → Circle split vertically half-black half-white; NEW POLE → Small hollow open circle; EXISTING COLUMN → Small solid black filled square; NEW COLUMN → Small hollow open square; EXISTING PILLAR → Solid black filled rectangle; NEW PILLAR → Hollow rectangle outline; PADMOUNT SUBSTATION → Rectangle containing two triangles touching at their points (bowtie shape); POLE SUBSTATION → Circle with triangle inside; LV LINK (N/O) → Small circle with short vertical ticks on top and bottom; HV ABS (N/C) → Circle with horizontal line through the centre; HV USL (N/C) → Semi-circle dome with horizontal base line; HIGH PRESSURE GAS → Solid line with GAS text centred along it; WATER MAIN → Solid line with W characters spaced evenly along it; LGA DEMARCATION → Thin solid line; NEW FREEWAY BOUNDARY → Faint light grey long dashed line; ENDEAVOUR ENERGY EASEMENT → Faint light grey medium dashed line. When you see a symbol that matches one of these, use the reference description exactly.
sub_steps.output_format:
JSON array of legend entry objects. Each object must have: 'page' (integer, 1-based), 'symbol_description' (string — visual geometry only: shape, fill, line weight, pattern, interior marks — never a label paraphrase), 'label' (string — verbatim label text from drawing), 'category' (string — one of: 'cable', 'equipment', 'substation', 'boundary', 'annotation', 'earthing', 'other'). Each row in the legend must produce a UNIQUE symbol_description — if two consecutive entries have identical symbol_descriptions you have made an error and must re-examine each row. Example output:
[
  { "page": 1, "symbol_description": "Long dashed line", "label": "NEW LV TRENCH", "category": "cable" },
  { "page": 1, "symbol_description": "Solid thin line", "label": "EXISTING OH CABLE", "category": "cable" },
  { "page": 1, "symbol_description": "Dotted line", "label": "REMOVE CONDUCTOR", "category": "cable" },
  { "page": 1, "symbol_description": "Thick heavy solid black line", "label": "EXISTING DUCTS", "category": "cable" },
  { "page": 1, "symbol_description": "Small hollow open circle", "label": "NEW POLE", "category": "equipment" },
  { "page": 1, "symbol_description": "Small solid black filled circle", "label": "EXISTING POLE", "category": "equipment" },
  { "page": 1, "symbol_description": "Small solid black filled circle with large X through it", "label": "REMOVE POLE", "category": "equipment" },
  { "page": 1, "symbol_description": "Circle with starburst outer spikes and hollow centre", "label": "NEW LANTERN", "category": "equipment" },
  { "page": 1, "symbol_description": "Circle with internal cross and starburst outer spikes", "label": "REMOVE LANTERN", "category": "equipment" },
  { "page": 1, "symbol_description": "Hollow rectangle outline", "label": "NEW PILLAR", "category": "equipment" },
  { "page": 1, "symbol_description": "Rectangle containing two triangles touching at their points", "label": "PADMOUNT SUBSTATION", "category": "substation" },
  { "page": 1, "symbol_description": "Thin solid line", "label": "LGA DEMARCATION", "category": "boundary" },
  { "page": 1, "symbol_description": "Solid line with W characters spaced evenly along it", "label": "WATER MAIN", "category": "other" }
]

instructional_sub_steps.title: Locate the Legend Area
instructional_sub_steps.instructions: Scan the drawing for a box, panel, or area labelled 'LEGEND', 'KEY', 'NOTATION', 'SYMBOL KEY', or 'DRAWING LEGEND'. This is typically found in the bottom-right or bottom-left corner of the drawing, within or adjacent to the title block. On multi-page drawings, the legend may appear only on page 1 — if so, note it as page 1. If no dedicated legend box is found, check for an unlabelled key or symbol table embedded in the drawing.

instructional_sub_steps.title: Describe Each Symbol Individually — Each Row Has Its Own Unique Symbol
instructional_sub_steps.instructions: CRITICAL: Every row in the legend has its own distinct drawn symbol. Each symbol_description must be unique — if you find yourself writing the same description for two or more consecutive rows, you have misread the legend and must look again. For LINE symbols: describe the line pattern precisely — solid/dashed/dotted/chain-dashed, thin/thick/heavy, and any decorations spaced along it (tick marks, letters like W or GAS, diagonal slashes, arrows). For POINT/ICON symbols: describe the outer shape (circle/square/rectangle/triangle), fill (hollow/solid black/half-filled), and any interior marks (cross, X, diagonal line, triangle, bowtie). Use the REFERENCE TABLE in the details field when you recognise a known symbol — match it and use the reference description. Ask yourself before writing each entry: 'Is this description unique and geometric? Could a reader draw exactly this symbol from my words alone without reading the label?'

instructional_sub_steps.title: Copy the Label Verbatim
instructional_sub_steps.instructions: For the 'label' field, copy the exact text written next to the symbol on the drawing, preserving capitalisation and spacing. Do not paraphrase or abbreviate. If a symbol has no label, record label as '[UNLABELLED]'. If a label has no visible symbol, record symbol_description as '[NO SYMBOL]'.

instructional_sub_steps.title: Assign Category
instructional_sub_steps.instructions: For each entry, assign a category based on the label text: 'cable' for any cable, conductor, trench, or service line; 'equipment' for poles, pillars, columns, lanterns, service points, switches, fuses, or meters; 'substation' for padmount, kiosk, or pole substation types; 'boundary' for lot boundaries, easement lines, LGA demarcation, or property lines; 'earthing' for earth conductors or earth electrodes; 'annotation' for text labels, callout arrows, or dimensions; 'other' for anything else.

instructional_sub_steps.title: Tag to Page Number
instructional_sub_steps.instructions: Tag each entry with the page number from the chunk metadata. If the same legend appears on multiple pages, record it once tagged to page 1. If different pages have different or updated legends, record separate entries per page.
